_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing matching and merging rules for entity resolution, including rule documentation, threshold calibration, and exception handling workflows.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI DQ domain. DQ-S-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RM-T-002 (RM, cosine: 0.320), DQ-A-002 (DQ, cosine: 0.317).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.4/10 (raw 0.344). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [deconfliction, facilitate, target] vs WIDAI-only terms [calibration, designing, documentation, entity, exception, handling] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.842 (P=0.849, R=0.835). Balanced P/R — the 3-word DCWF element and 19-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Facilitate target deconfliction" and "Skill in designing matching and merging rules for entity resolution, including r" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.342 ≈ STS 0.344 (gap 0.003). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [none] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [deconfliction, facilitate, target]. WIDAI terms: [calibration, designing, documentation, entity, exception, handling].
